body_txt: A character with a freakish appearance, experiencing loneliness, happens to stumble into a blind person who doesn't realize that the freak is not quite human . They strike up a friendship or even romance, and the audience learns An Aesop that blind people might be better than we are because they are quicker to recognize inner beauty . Naturally, this situation carries some improbabilities which tend to be ignored for the narrative's sake — the less humanoid the freak is, the harder should it be for the blind person not to perceive his inhumanity through non-visual cues like touch and feel, the sound of his voice or his movements in the space, or just his behavior. In any case, usually the deception doesn't last forever, but by the time the blind person finds out what the other "looks" like, they've already come to appreciate the person inside. Compare Freaky Is Cool , Unseen Pen Pal .
